page: Amazon Web Services inf1.6xlarge is an Intel-based instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs, representing 12 physical cores with hyperthreading, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the system is equipped with 48.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or discrete GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position the inf1.6xlarge in the average tier for general compute tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1259.0 and a multi-core score of 9635.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 23788.28. Its memory subsystem demonstrates strong performance, with a PassMark memory score of 2769.15, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems, and a read-only cached memory bandwidth of 1315.54 GB/sec. For language model workloads, the instance processes 1141.9 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model, dropping to 36.03 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the inf1.6xlarge offers a compute-leaning resource profile. The lack of local storage requires workloads to rely entirely on network-attached storage, which is supported by the 25 Gbps network interface. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated Intel Xeon compute power without massive memory footprints. It is suited for web serving, medium-scale database operations, software compilation, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services inf1.6xlarge is a compute-oriented instance featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 2.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers balanced compute performance with a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. It contains no local storage or discrete GPUs. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory performance in the average tier, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 23788.28 and a strong PassMark memory score in the top 25%. The instance is suited for web serving, software compilation, and small to medium model inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.6xlarge is an Intel Xeon-powered instance with 24 dedicated vCPUs, 48 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for compute-heavy workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf1.6xlarge features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated Intel Xeon compute instance with balanced memory performance and high-speed networking for optimized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 48 GB DDR4 RAM
Nitro hypervisor integration with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong memory performance, scoring in the top 25% on PassMark memory benchmarks
No local storage or discrete GPUs included in the hardware profile
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale database operations
